description_long: This object is a vintage analog synthesizer keyboard, featuring a wooden chassis with a black control panel and a set of white and black piano-style keys. The control panel is populated with numerous rotary knobs, sliders, and toggle switches, many of which are accented in blue and orange, indicating their various functions for sound modulation and synthesis. The left side of the keyboard includes pitch and modulation wheels, while the right side is dominated by the main keyboard section. The top panel is adorned with labeled sections for oscillators, filters, and envelope controls, all clearly marked for user interaction. The wood grain finish gives the instrument a classic, high-quality appearance, and the overall design is reminiscent of iconic synthesizers from the late 20th century. This keyboard would likely be used in music studios, performance venues, or as a street instrument in urban environments.
description: A vintage analog synthesizer keyboard with a wooden frame, black control panel, and numerous knobs, sliders, and switches for sound control.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full keyboard, control panel with labeled sections, and pitch/modulation wheels on the left.
description_view_1: Left-angled view highlighting the pitch and modulation wheels and the depth of the wooden chassis.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the control panel layout and the back edge of the keyboard.
description_view_3: Right-angled view emphasizing the keyboard section and the side profile of the wooden frame.
attribute_key_layout: Piano-style synthesizer keybed (approx. 37 keys, no numpad)
